rationale: This reaction shows the decarbonylation of a simple, acyclic aliphatic aldehyde (heptanal) to its corresponding alkane (hexane) using Wilkinson's catalyst. This is a classic textbook example that clearly illustrates the C-C bond cleavage of the formyl group and its replacement with a C-H bond, which is the core transformation of the Tsuji-Wilkinson decarbonylation. The substrate is simple and avoids other functional groups that could cause ambiguity.
collision_avoidance_note: The use of a simple, saturated aliphatic aldehyde avoids overlap with reactions involving aromatic aldehydes (which could be confused with various named condensations or reductions) or acyl halides (which could be confused with Rosenmund reduction). It specifically avoids motifs common in decarboxylation (carboxylic acids), deoxygenation (ketones/alcohols), or radical reactions, focusing purely on the deformylation of an aldehyde to an alkane.
